topic: Someone discovers their partner has been writing a novel about them
raw_output:
[story 1]

The aroma of old paper and strong coffee hung heavy in the air of our apartment. I was just trying to find the remote, but my eyes snagged on the laptop sitting open on the kitchen counter. It was Mark’s laptop. Hesitantly, I approached, my heart beginning a frantic, uneven rhythm against my ribs. The screen displayed a document titled, "The Unfolding: A Chronicle of Elara." My breath hitched. The first paragraph, typed in his familiar, thoughtful hand, was a description of my morning routine—the way I brewed tea, the slight furrow in my brow when I was concentrating on spreadsheets. A wave of heat washed over my face, starting at my neck and creeping up to my hairline. I backed away slowly, bumping into the refrigerator. "Mark?" My voice came out thin, reedy. He looked up, a small, pleased smile playing on his lips. "Just working on a project," he said, his tone completely unconcerned. The reality of what I’d stumbled upon settled over me like a physical weight, making my hands tremble uncontrollably.

[story 2]

I found the evidence tucked beneath a stack of graphic novels on his bedside table. It was a leather-bound notebook, filled with elegant script and detailed character sketches. My stomach clenched, a cold knot tightening in my core. I flipped through the pages, and there, meticulously detailed, was my life. Every embarrassing anecdote, every quiet moment of vulnerability, every shared glance—it was all there, rendered in prose. A sudden, sharp dizziness overtook me. I squeezed my eyes shut, trying to physically shunt the images away, but they clung to my vision. When Mark walked in, whistling, I quickly slammed the book shut, my movements jerky and too fast. "What are you doing?" he asked, pausing in the doorway. I managed a strained, brittle laugh. "Nothing! Just… tidying up. Old papers." I kept my gaze fixed firmly on the floor, acutely aware of the way my cheeks felt burning hot. The silence stretched, thick with unspoken knowledge, and I desperately wished the floor would swallow me whole.

[story 3]

It happened during a late-night browsing session. I was looking for a movie recommendation, and I clicked on a shared folder on his cloud drive. The file name, "My Muse," made my fingers freeze over the mouse. Curiosity, sharp and unwelcome, pulled me in. Inside, it wasn't just notes; it was chapters. Chapters written in his voice, yet describing my anxieties, my secret hopes, the way I reacted to stress. A strange, suffocating pressure built behind my sternum. I leaned closer to the screen, my knuckles white where I gripped the edge of the desk. When he finally appeared, looking for a snack, I yanked the laptop shut with unnecessary force. The sudden noise made him jump. "Whoa, what's wrong?" he inquired, concern coloring his tone. I swallowed hard, tasting copper in my mouth. I stood up too quickly, my legs feeling unsteady, and pointed vaguely at the closed lid. "Nothing. Just… a really intense article I was reading," I lied, my voice sounding unnaturally high. I spent the next hour staring blankly at the wall, every muscle tense, waiting for the inevitable moment he’d realize the truth.
